strategy: Directly inspect the 01:20-01:25 interval for all visible price-bearing items, read the prices with OCR, then use grounded interpretation to total only the items whose price points are actually visible in that window.

steps.purpose: Retrieve representative frames from 01:20-01:25 that show the shopping/product pages and any visible prices.

arguments.query: shopping or product webpage frames in 01:20-01:25 where dollar prices are visible on screen
time_hints: frames where product listings or product detail pages with dollar prices are visible
include beginning, middle, and end of the interval

steps.purpose: Read all visible text and dollar amounts from the retrieved frames in the target interval.

arguments.query: Read all visible item names and dollar prices in these frames, especially any dollar amounts associated with products.

steps.purpose: Determine which distinct items in 01:20-01:25 have visible price points and compute their total from the grounded frame and OCR evidence.

arguments.query: Using only these frames and OCR results from 01:20-01:25, identify every distinct item that has a visible price point on screen during this interval, list each item's visible price once, exclude duplicate appearances of the same item across frames, and compute the total cost.

refinement_instructions: Preserve the preprocess anchor that the relevant visual content is the shopping/product-page montage around 68-73s, but do not rely on preprocess alone because the question requires exact visible prices and a total. Replace any unsupported assumptions about which items are counted with grounded evidence from frames in 01:20-01:25 only. The new evidence must resolve: (1) which distinct items have visible dollar prices in that interval, (2) the exact price text for each such item, and (3) the summed total after deduplicating repeated views of the same item. If OCR/frame evidence does not make the prices legible enough to total decisively, the answer should remain unresolved rather than guessed.
